report:
{'type': 'thinking', 'thinking': "Okay, let's work through this step by step. First, I need to audit the code against the SPEC test cases. Let's go through each TC one by one.

First, let's list the TCs and check each against the provided code.

Starting with TC-01: Registry - Project Listing. The ProjectList component uses /api/asf/projects/list, maps over projects, shows ID, Name, Path. That's covered.

TC-02: Setup - Category×Level Auto-Upgrade. The ProjectSetup has handleLCOnChange that calls /api/asf/pc/defaults, sets team and process. Also, rules have auto_upgrades for VIZ L3 → T3/P3. The backend's get_pc_defaults does that. So that's covered.

TC-03: Setup - INFRA Hard Constraint. Backend's get_pc_defaults sets level to L3 if INFRA and rank <3, and team=T5, process=P5. Also, init_project has hard rules for INFRA. But wait, does the frontend handle it? The backend will return 400 if violated, but also get_pc_defaults auto-upgrades. So that's acceptable per SPEC note.

TC-04: Setup - Team↔Process Bidirectional. ProjectSetup has handleTChange (team→process: replace T with P) and handlePChange (process→team: replace P with T). That's covered.

TC-05: Setup - Create Project Success. ProjectSetup's onFinish sends POST to /api/asf/projects/create, then navigates to /projects/{id}. Backend has create_project which calls init_project. That's covered.

TC-06: Dashboard - Level Selector. ProjectDashboard has a Select for Level in Infrastructure Matrix with L1-L5. Yes, that's there.

TC-07: Dashboard - Category Change Triggers Defaults. ProjectDashboard's Category Select onChange calls /api/asf/pc/defaults and updates team. Yes, that's in the code.

TC-08: Dashboard - T↔P Bidirectional Sync. ProjectDashboard's Team Select onChange sets process to replace T with P, and Process Select onChange sets team (checks if TEAM_DEFS has the replaced P→T, otherwise keeps current). That's covered.

TC-09: Workstation - Project List Load. Workstation uses /api/asf/projects/list to populate the Select. Yes, fetchProjects is called.

TC-10: Workstation - Event Polling. Workstation has useEffect that polls /api/asf/projects/{id}/events/take every 2s, updates logs and nodes. Yes.

TC-11: Backend - pc_rules.json Valid. Backend has /api/asf/pc/rules which returns mgr.rules, which loads pc_rules.json. The rules file is provided, has all sections.

TC-12: Backend - Defaults API. Backend's get_pc_defaults for INFRA L3 returns team=T5, process=P5. Yes, the code has that hard rule.

Wait, let's double-check each one. Also, check if there are any gaps.

Wait, let's check TC-03: in get_pc_defaults, if category is INFRA, it sets team=T5, process=P5, and if level rank <3, sets level to L3. Then init_project has hard rules that check, but get_pc_defaults auto-upgrades, so that's acceptable per SPEC note (either error or auto-upgrade).

Another check: TC-04 in Setup: handleTChange replaces T with P, handlePChange replaces P with T. Yes. And in Dashboard, same: Team Select onChange replaces T with P for process, Process Select onChange replaces P with T for team (if TEAM_DEFS has it).

Wait, in ProjectDashboard's Team Select: when you change team, it calls updateState to set meta.process to v.replace('T', 'P'). And Process Select onChange sets team.structure to TEAM_DEFS[teamId]? teamId is v.replace('P', 'T'), then if TEAM_DEFS has that, uses it, else keeps current. Yes, that's there.

Also, pc_rules.json is valid, has all sections (categories, levels, teams, processes, constraints).

Workstation's event polling: yes, every 2s calls take events, updates logs and node styles.

Okay, all TCs seem to be covered. So decision is PASS? Wait, let's make sure.

Wait, let's check if there's any missing. Let's see:

TC-02: Setup, VIZ L3 → T3/P3. The backend's auto_upgrades has that, and handleLCOnChange calls the defaults API, so yes.

TC-07: Dashboard, change Category to VIZ → Team T3. Yes, the Category Select's onChange calls /api/asf/pc/defaults and updates team.

Yes, all TCs are covered. So decision is PASS."}
